evals.prompt: Design a GraphQL schema for a project management app. It needs users, projects, and tasks. Users can be assigned to projects and tasks. Tasks belong to a project and have statuses (todo, in progress, done). Users can create projects, create tasks, assign tasks, and change task status.
evals.expected_output: A complete GraphQL schema in SDL following best practices: noun-based queries with filters and connections, verb+noun mutations with single input args and payload unions, custom scalars for IDs, proper nullability, Error interface, documented types.

assertions.name: uses-custom-scalars
assertions.check: Schema defines custom scalar types for IDs (e.g., UserId, ProjectId, TaskId) instead of bare ID

assertions.name: noun-based-queries
assertions.check: Query fields use noun naming (user, users, project) not RPC-style (getUserById)

assertions.name: verb-noun-mutations
assertions.check: Mutations use verb+noun naming (createProject, assignTask) not generic CRUD (updateTask)

assertions.name: single-input-pattern
assertions.check: All mutations use (input: XxxInput!) pattern

assertions.name: payload-unions
assertions.check: Mutations return union payload types with success and error members

assertions.name: connection-pagination
assertions.check: Unbounded lists use Relay-style connection types

assertions.name: error-interface
assertions.check: Defines an Error interface with message and code fields

assertions.name: schema-documentation
assertions.check: Types and fields have triple-quote description strings

assertions.name: no-foreign-key-ids
assertions.check: No xxxId fields alongside relationship object fields

evals.prompt: Review this GraphQL schema and tell me what's wrong with it and how to fix it.
evals.expected_output: A structured review identifying anti-patterns with concrete fixes.

assertions.name: identifies-rpc-naming
assertions.check: Flags RPC-style query names (getUserById, getAllProjects, etc.)

assertions.name: identifies-crud-mutations
assertions.check: Flags generic CRUD mutations (updateUser, updateTask) as lacking business intent and recommends domain-specific replacements

assertions.name: identifies-missing-input-types
assertions.check: Flags positional mutation arguments

assertions.name: identifies-foreign-key-ids
assertions.check: Flags foreign key ID fields that duplicate relationship fields

assertions.name: identifies-bare-types
assertions.check: Flags String used for timestamps, statuses, roles, or IDs

assertions.name: identifies-missing-pagination
assertions.check: Flags unbounded lists that should use connections

assertions.name: identifies-missing-error-handling
assertions.check: Flags mutations returning bare types or Boolean instead of payload unions

assertions.name: fixes-use-payload-unions
assertions.check: Corrected schema code uses payload union types (union keyword with success + error members), not a userErrors array pattern

evals.prompt: We have this ticket tracking schema. I need to add a 'time tracking' feature — users should be able to log time entries against tickets, and we need to query total time spent per ticket and per user. Please add the new types and mutations.
evals.expected_output: New types and mutations that follow best practices even though the existing schema does not — custom scalars, domain mutations, payload unions, connections.

assertions.name: uses-custom-scalars-for-new-types
assertions.check: Defines custom scalar IDs for new entities (e.g., TimeEntryId) instead of bare ID, even though the existing schema uses bare ID

assertions.name: domain-mutations-not-crud
assertions.check: Time tracking mutations use domain-specific names (e.g., logTimeEntry, not createTimeEntry/updateTimeEntry) with single input pattern

assertions.name: payload-unions-for-new-mutations
assertions.check: New mutations return payload union types with success and error members, even though existing mutations return bare types

assertions.name: error-interface
assertions.check: Defines or references an Error interface with message and code fields for error types

assertions.name: connection-for-time-entries
assertions.check: Time entries on tickets use a connection type for pagination, not a bare list

assertions.name: does-not-propagate-anti-patterns
assertions.check: New code does not follow the existing schema's anti-patterns (no bare ID for new types, no positional args, no bare return types)

evals.prompt: I'm using Pothos with TypeScript and Prisma. I need to add a 'workspace invitations' feature — workspace admins can invite users by email, invitees can accept or decline. An invitation expires after 7 days. Show me the Pothos type definitions.
evals.expected_output: Pothos builder code with typed IDs, domain mutations (inviteUserToWorkspace, acceptInvitation, declineInvitation), payload unions via Pothos union/error patterns, and proper TypeScript types.

assertions.name: uses-pothos-builder
assertions.check: Output uses Pothos SchemaBuilder API (builder.objectType, builder.mutationField, builder.unionType, etc.)

assertions.name: domain-mutations
assertions.check: Mutations are domain-specific (inviteUserToWorkspace, acceptInvitation, declineInvitation), not CRUD (createInvitation, updateInvitation)

assertions.name: payload-unions-in-pothos
assertions.check: Mutations return union types defined via Pothos (builder.unionType or equivalent) with success and error members

assertions.name: invitation-state-modeling
assertions.check: Invitation status is modeled with proper types — either an enum with expiration logic or a union/status field that distinguishes pending/accepted/declined/expired

assertions.name: typescript-types
assertions.check: Includes TypeScript type definitions or interfaces alongside Pothos definitions
